one_time_description: Logging income of $2,799,052 appears for the first time in 2022 (nil in 2021 budget and prior year), representing a significant non-recurring revenue from the Nuchatlaht First Nation Community Logging Corporation
economic_notes: Nuchatlaht First Nation owns 25% interest in Hayu Fishing Limited Partnership and 25% interest in Hayu Fishing Ltd. (general partner). Also owns 1075124 B.C. Ltd. and Nuchatlaht First Nation Community Logging Corporation (fully consolidated). Logging generated $2,799,052 in revenue in FY2022.
notes: Consolidated entity includes Nuchatlaht First Nation government, Social Housing Program, Trust Fund, Hayu Fishing Limited Partnership (25% modified equity), Hayu Fishing Ltd. (25% modified equity), 1075124 B.C. Ltd. (full consolidation), and Nuchatlaht First Nation Community Logging Corporation (full consolidation). ISC funding is received via Nuu-chah-nulth Tribal Council and not separately identified. Total government transfers estimated as Nuu-chah-nulth Tribal Council operating ($1,379,936) + Province of BC ($387,016) + Union of BC Municipalities ($93,725) + First Nations Education Steering Committee ($89,002) + First Nations Health Authority ($70,000) + Department of Fisheries and Oceans ($27,125) + CMHC subsidies ($47,303) + NDN Collective ($123,776) = $2,217,883 approximately; used $2,238,779 including Social Housing rents ($21,420) and DFO. Logging income is a new major revenue source in FY2022 with no prior year comparative. No remuneration schedule included in the provided pages.
